name: Viveiro – tour around Paseo Marítimo and Ría de Viveiro
name_original: Viveiro – vuelta por Paseo Marítimo y Ría de Viveiro
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Viveiro offer a varied landscape where the Landro river meets the Cantabrian Sea, forming the Ría de Viveiro. The region features a mix of coastal paths, estuary-side trails, and inland routes that include both paved surfaces and some unpaved sections. Cyclists can explore the flat expanses along the estuary or venture into areas with considerable elevation gains, including hills like Monte Faro. This diverse terrain provides options for various fitness levels, from easy rides to challenging long-distance expeditions. Best touring cycling routes around Viveiro The most popular touring cycling route is Viveiro – tour around Paseo Marítimo and Ría de Viveiro, a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route offers views along the Viveiro estuary, primarily on paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mariña de Celeiro – Covas loop from A Casanova, an easy 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This loop provides a coastal experience with minimal elevation gain, connecting the fishing port of Celeiro with Covas beach. Local touring cyclists also love the View of O Porto de Bares – O Porto do Barqueiro loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro, a 32.3 miles (52.0 km) trail leading through coastal and inland areas with significant climbs, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Viveiro is defined by coastal paths, the Ría de Viveiro estuary, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides along the promenade to more strenuous routes with considerable elevation. The routes in Viveiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Viveiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Viveiro?
A: Viveiro offers a diverse selection of over 30 touring cycling routes. These range from easy rides along the estuary to challenging long-distance expeditions with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Viveiro?
A: The terrain around Viveiro is quite varied, featuring a mix of coastal paths, estuary-side trails along the Ría de Viveiro, and inland routes. You'll find mostly paved surfaces, especially on easier routes, but some longer, more challenging rides may include unpaved sections and considerable climbs, such as those leading up to Monte Faro.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Viveiro?
A: Yes, Viveiro has several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017392599/viveiro-tour-around-paseo-maritimo-and-ria-de-viveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viveiro – tour around Paseo Marítimo and Ría de Viveiro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 13.8 km trail that is mostly flat and paved, offering scenic views along the estuary. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441231" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mariña de Celeiro – Covas loop from A Casanova&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a coastal experience with minimal elevation.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along touring cycling routes in Viveiro?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural sights. You can cycle along the beautiful &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Viveiro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Viveiro&lt;/a&gt;, explore various beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3727364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Román Beach&lt;/a&gt;, or visit viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tixoso Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for spectacular sea views. Further afield, you might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paper Cliffs&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by waterfalls or unique forests?
A: Yes, some routes venture inland where you can discover natural wonders. The area around Chavín is known for its spectacular eucalyptus forests, including 'El Abuelo,' one of Spain's tallest trees. Nestled within this environment is the picturesque Pozo da Ferida waterfall, which makes for a refreshing stop.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Viveiro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Viveiro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied landscapes, from serene estuary paths to challenging coastal climbs, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical towns.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Viveiro?
A: Yes, Viveiro offers several circular routes. For a challenging coastal and inland experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165430" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of O Porto de Bares – O Porto do Barqueiro loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro&lt;/a&gt;, a 52 km trail with significant climbs. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Illa de Sarón – Paper Cliffs loop from Auga Doce&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 38 km route.

Q: Can I find historical landmarks or interesting towns along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Viveiro itself is a town with a rich medieval past, featuring preserved ancient walls and gates like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt;. The fishing port of Celeiro is also a point of interest with historical buildings. Further along the coast, you might pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Porto do Barqueiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is it possible to cycle with dogs on the touring routes in Viveiro?
A: While many outdoor areas in Viveiro are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific beaches or protected natural areas. Generally, dogs on a leash are welcome on most public paths and trails. Ensure your dog is fit for the chosen route's distance and elevation, and always carry water for them.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging touring cycling expeditions from Viveiro?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Viveiro serves as a starting point for significant cycling journeys. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31441828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covas – Mariña de Celeiro loop from O Folgueiro&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 35 km with considerable elevation, offer a good test. The region also connects to longer routes like the Camino Natural del Cantábrico, which can be explored in segments.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Viveiro?
A: The coastal climate of Viveiro generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling from spring through early autumn. During these months, temperatures are milder, and rainfall is typically lower, making it ideal for exploring both the coastal and inland routes. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.
tips: Public access recreational path. the promenade connects the estuary to Playa Covas and Vivero and reaches the Puente Mayor or Puente de la Misericordia.
This is an arched stone gate to the old city. Travelers who crossed the estuary via the Mayor or La Misericordia bridges reached the entrance to the old town.
A nice trail runs along the marina, which is directly on the ria de Viveiro, but also quite well protected by a jetty on both sides
Large historic Franciscan convent with an imposing church tower.
Main East gate of the city, probably held smaller to be better defensible as it faces the inland area.

Church and Convent of San Francisco de Viveiro
